summary: This passage from Reginald Scot's 'Discoverie of Witchcraft' details various juggling tricks and 'knacks'—such as the colored book, pulling laces from the mouth, and simulated beheadings—to demonstrate that what appears to be supernatural magic is actually clever manual dexterity and mechanical deception. Scot aims to debunk the belief in witchcraft by exposing the 'legerdemaine' of performers through technical descriptions of their methods.
